ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Kadak Roti; closest parent 'flatbread food product' (FOODON_03302397) used as the ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000E (Flatbread) is the closest defensible parent category for Kadak Roti; no specific code exists for this crispy Indian flatbread variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 'Flatbread (food)' used as closest parent; no specific code for Kadak Roti exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Kadak Roti typically weighs approximately 40 grams and is served as a single crispy flatbread alongside curry, dal, or chutney.

nutrient_source.title: Chapati (Roti), whole wheat, cooked — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Kadak Roti is a crispy, dry-roasted variant of standard whole wheat roti/chapati. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for whole wheat chapati (cooked). Moisture content is lower in Kadak Roti due to extended dry cooking; values adjusted proportionally for reduced moisture (~5% vs ~38% in standard chapati) to reflect the drier, crispier product. Energy and macros scaled accordingly. Where ICMR-NIN data was not available for specific micronutrients, USDA SR Legacy (Bread, whole-wheat, commercially prepared, NDB 18069) was used as a secondary reference.

gi_source.title: Chapati, whole wheat flour — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kadak Roti specifically. GI of 62 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for whole wheat chapati/roti (GI range 52–72, representative value 62). Kadak Roti uses the same whole wheat flour base but is cooked longer and drier; the reduced moisture and increased crispiness may slightly lower the GI relative to standard chapati, but 62 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 27.0) / 100 = 16.7, where carbohydrate per 40 g serving = 67.5 × 0.40 = 27.0 g.

hypertension.guidance: Kadak Roti contains approximately 320 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or salted chutneys to keep total meal sodium within the recommended 1500–2000 mg/day target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and high carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 62 and 67.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g, Kadak Roti has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit serving size to one roti (40 g) and pair it with high-fibre vegetables, legumes, or protein-rich dal to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Whole wheat composition and low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kadak Roti made from whole wheat flour provides 6.5 g dietary fibre per 100 g and only 1.2 g saturated fat, supporting a heart-healthy dietary pattern; however, sodium content should be monitored and ghee-enriched versions should be consumed sparingly by individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kadak Roti is a crispy, robust Indian flatbread made from whole wheat flour (atta), water, and a small amount of salt, cooked on a dry tawa (griddle) or in a tandoor until it becomes firm and crunchy — the word 'kadak' meaning 'hard' or 'crispy' in Hindi. Unlike the soft, pliable standard chapati or phulka, Kadak Roti is cooked longer and at higher heat, driving off most of the moisture to produce a rigid, cracker-like texture that holds up well when dipped into thick curries, dal, or sabzi. It is a staple in North Indian and Punjabi cuisine and is commonly served in Indian restaurants in Singapore as an alternative to naan or soft roti. Nutritionally, Kadak Roti is a good source of complex carbohydrates (approximately 67.5 g per 100 g), dietary fibre (6.5 g per 100 g from whole wheat bran), and plant-based protein (10.8 g per 100 g). Its fat content is low (4.5 g per 100 g) when prepared without added ghee. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 62 (medium GI), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for individuals with hypertension. Kadak Roti is naturally vegan, vegetarian, and halal when prepared without dairy-based ghee, and is free from cholesterol. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten or wheat allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact ICMR-NIN or HPB entry exists specifically for Kadak Roti. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN whole wheat chapati data with moisture-adjusted scaling to reflect the drier, crispier product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for whole wheat chapati. Confidence is medium due to the adaptation rather than a direct match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN whole wheat chapati (cooked) data, scaled for the lower moisture content (~12% water) characteristic of Kadak Roti compared to standard chapati (~38% water). 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for whole wheat flatbreads. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for whole wheat flatbreads. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for a minimally processed whole wheat flatbread with no hydrogenated fats. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based flatbread with no animal-derived ingredients in the base recipe. 8. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; whole wheat flatbreads contain negligible vitamin C. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 10. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for whole wheat chapati; no direct GI measurement for Kadak Roti was found in the approved sources. 11. Dietary flags assume the base recipe without ghee; ghee-enriched versions would not be vegan or dairy-free. 12. Served at Anjana Kitchen - Six Battery Road on field survey data at SGD 6.75.

improvement_actions_taken: Populated all mandatory multilingual local_names for Indian cuisine in Singapore context (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Adapted nutrient values from ICMR-NIN whole wheat chapati data with moisture-adjusted scaling for Kadak Roti's drier profile.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-based justifications in notes.
Documented all 0.0 values (trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg) with confirmed zero justifications in notes.
Set GI to 62 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for whole wheat chapati; gi_category correctly set to 'medium' per 56–69 threshold.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving as 16.7 for 40 g serving.
Used only closest_parent match_type values for all three ontology systems with gap notes; no invented codes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases.
Included all three health conditions with specific clinical guidance sentences; Type 2 Diabetes uses human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key 'type2_diabetes'.
